pattern: Building a count n by copy-then-paste decomposes into prime factors: the minimum operation count equals the sum of the prime factors of n.
hints: Think about what sequence of copies and pastes reaches exactly n characters, and how each copy resets what a paste duplicates.
If you grow from a block of size d that divides the target, the cost to multiply your current size by a factor f is exactly f operations (one copy plus f-1 pastes).
Repeatedly divide n by its smallest factor greater than one and add each factor to the answer; this is the same as summing n's prime factors.

edge_inputs.description: single character already present needs no operations
prime requires that many single pastes
composite better than treating as prime

stress.description: all values across the full range
